course_description: Bốn bài nói A1 (British Council): kiểm tra hiểu biết, đề xuất, gặp gỡ người mới, nói về người khác.

preparation_pages.title: Chuẩn bị (Preparation)
preparation_pages.body:
### Các cụm từ kiểm tra hiểu biết (Checking Understanding Phrases)

Dùng những cụm từ này khi bạn không nghe rõ hoặc không hiểu người khác nói gì:

- **I'm sorry?** — Xin lỗi? (yêu cầu nhắc lại)
- **I can't hear you very well.** — Tôi không nghe rõ lắm.
- **Could you say that again, please?** — Bạn có thể nói lại được không?
- **I don't understand.** — Tôi không hiểu.
- **Can you repeat that more slowly, please?** — Bạn có thể nhắc lại chậm hơn không?
- **Do you mean ...?** — Ý bạn là ...?
- **Is this it?** — Đây có phải cái đó không?
- **Yes, that's right!** — Đúng vậy!

> **Mẹo học:** Những cụm từ này rất hữu ích trong các tình huống nghe không rõ hoặc cần xác nhận thông tin.

content.reading_passage:
## Đoạn hội thoại mẫu: Checking Understanding

**Bối cảnh:** Paul và Bob đang làm việc. Bob muốn Paul đưa cho mình một dụng cụ, nhưng Paul không nghe rõ.

---

**Bob:** This isn't right. Hey! Excuse me, Paul, could you pass me the hammer?

**Paul:** Sorry, Bob, my ears are blocked. I can't hear you very well. Could you say that again, please?

**Bob:** The hammer. Please could you pass it to me?

**Paul:** The spanner?

**Bob:** No. The hammer!

**Paul:** Sorry, Bob, I don't understand. This is the spanner!

**Bob:** No, Paul, I need the hammer. It's in the toolbox, on the left, under the scissors.

**Paul:** Sorry, Bob. Could you repeat that more slowly, please?

**Bob:** The hammer — in the toolbox — on the left — under the scissors.

**Paul:** On the left — under the scissors — Oh! Do you mean the hammer? Is this it?

**Bob:** Yes, that's right!

**Paul:** Ah! Why didn't you say so?

**Bob:** I did!

---

### Các cụm từ nổi bật trong hội thoại:

| Cụm từ | Nghĩa |
|--------|-------|
| I can't hear you very well. | Tôi không nghe rõ lắm. |
| Could you say that again, please? | Bạn có thể nói lại không? |
| I don't understand. | Tôi không hiểu. |
| Could you repeat that more slowly? | Bạn có thể nhắc lại chậm hơn không? |
| Do you mean the hammer? | Ý bạn là cái búa? |
| Is this it? | Đây có phải không? |
| Yes, that's right! | Đúng rồi! |

quizzes.question_text: Paul says 'I can't hear you very well.' What does this mean?

options.A: Paul does not want to listen.
options.C: Paul is having difficulty hearing Bob.
options.D: Paul is asking Bob to speak louder only.

quizzes.hint_text: Paul's ears are blocked, so he is having trouble hearing.

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase do you use to ask someone to say something again?

options.B: Could you say that again, please?

quizzes.hint_text: This phrase directly asks the person to repeat what they said.

quizzes.question_text: Bob wants Paul to pass him the ___.
quizzes.hint_text: Bob clearly says the name of the tool he needs several times.

quizzes.question_text: Paul says 'Could you repeat that more slowly, please?' When should you use this phrase?

options.A: When you want someone to speak louder.
options.B: When someone is speaking too fast for you to understand.
options.C: When you want to end a conversation.
options.D: When you already understand but want confirmation.

quizzes.hint_text: The key word is 'slowly' — it is used when speech is too fast.

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase checks if you understood correctly by guessing?

options.D: I can't hear you very well.

quizzes.hint_text: 'Do you mean...?' is used to confirm your understanding by repeating what you think was said.

quizzes.question_text: Where is the hammer according to Bob?

options.A: In the toolbox, on the right, under the scissors.
options.B: In the toolbox, on the left, under the scissors.
options.C: In the toolbox, on the left, above the scissors.
options.D: Next to the toolbox, on the left.

quizzes.hint_text: Bob describes the location step by step.

quizzes.question_text: Paul picks up the wrong tool first. What does he pick up?
quizzes.hint_text: Paul says 'This is the spanner!' when Bob asks for the hammer.

quizzes.question_text: What does 'I'm sorry?' mean in a conversation?

options.C: I feel bad about this situation.
options.D: I am not available right now.

quizzes.hint_text: In conversation, 'I'm sorry?' (with rising intonation) is used to ask someone to repeat themselves.

quizzes.question_text: Which is the most polite way to ask someone to repeat something more slowly?

options.C: Could you repeat that more slowly, please?

quizzes.hint_text: Using 'Could you...please?' makes the request polite.

quizzes.question_text: After Bob explains the location again slowly, what does Paul say to confirm?

options.B: On the left — under the scissors — Oh! Do you mean the hammer? Is this it?
options.C: The hammer is in the toolbox.

quizzes.hint_text: Paul repeats the location and then checks 'Do you mean...? Is this it?'

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase is used to CONFIRM that someone has the right item?

options.A: Could you say that again, please?

quizzes.hint_text: 'Yes, that's right!' confirms that the other person has understood or found the correct thing.

quizzes.question_text: Your colleague gives you directions very quickly. Which TWO phrases could you use? Choose the BEST option.

options.A: I don't understand. / Yes, that's right!
options.B: Could you say that again? / Could you repeat that more slowly, please?
options.C: Is this it? / Do you mean...?
options.D: I can't hear you. / Yes, that's right!

quizzes.hint_text: When someone speaks too fast, you ask them to repeat and to slow down.

quizzes.question_text: Ana says: 'Try and use some of these phrases the next time you want to ___ your understanding in English.'
quizzes.hint_text: The lesson is about 'checking understanding'.

quizzes.question_text: Which sentence is INCORRECT for checking understanding?

options.A: Could you say that again, please?
options.D: I can't hear you very well.

quizzes.hint_text: Check the grammar — 'I am understand' is not correct English.

quizzes.question_text: Why does Paul have difficulty hearing Bob at the start of the conversation?
quizzes.hint_text: Paul says 'my ears are blocked' at the beginning.

quizzes.question_text: Which response is most appropriate when someone says 'Is this it?' and they have the correct item?
quizzes.hint_text: To confirm someone has the correct item, you say 'Yes, that's right!'

preparation_pages.title: Chuẩn bị (Preparation)
preparation_pages.body:
### Các cụm từ đề xuất (Making Suggestions Phrases)

Dùng những cụm từ này khi bạn muốn gợi ý hoặc đề xuất điều gì đó:

- **Let's ...** — Hãy cùng nhau ...
- **How about ...?** — Thế còn ... thì sao? / Chúng ta ... nhé?
- **Why don't you/we ...?** — Tại sao chúng ta không ...?
- **Shall we ...?** — Chúng ta có ... không?
- **Sure.** — Được thôi.
- **Agreed. Good idea.** — Đồng ý. Ý hay đấy.
- **OK, no problem.** — OK, không sao.
- **Good plan.** — Kế hoạch hay đấy.
- **Yeah, sure.** — Ừ, được thôi.
- **We'll need some extra help.** — Chúng ta sẽ cần thêm sự hỗ trợ.

> **Mẹo học:** 'Let's', 'How about', 'Why don't we', và 'Shall we' là những cách phổ biến nhất để đề xuất trong tiếng Anh hàng ngày.

content.reading_passage:
## Đoạn hội thoại mẫu: Making Suggestions

**Bối cảnh:** Noelia và Yuna đang họp. Họ tắt điện thoại để tập trung và đề xuất các kế hoạch công việc.

---

**Yuna:** OK, this time let's focus. Phones off.

**Noelia:** Yes, and in my bag!

**Yuna:** Ready?

**Noelia:** I'm ready.

**Yuna:** We have half an hour.

**Noelia:** OK, let's go!

**Yuna:** How about starting with social media?

**Noelia:** Sure. Engagement is up 15 per cent this month.

**Yuna:** Good. So let's go for 20 per cent for next month.

**Noelia:** Yeah, sure. I'll tell the team. We'll need some extra help to make it work.

**Yuna:** Agreed. Good idea.

**Yuna:** Why don't you ask Vanya to help you create a new social media plan?

**Noelia:** OK, no problem. Shall we present it at the next team meeting together?

**Yuna:** Good plan. Can you let Paul see it first?

**Noelia:** Sure, I'll do that.

**Yuna:** Anything else?

**Noelia:** No, that's it.

**Yuna:** And that's half an hour! We did it!

**Noelia:** We're really good at this!

---

### Các cụm từ đề xuất trong hội thoại:

| Cụm từ | Loại | Nghĩa |
|--------|------|-------|
| Let's focus. | Đề xuất (chúng ta) | Hãy tập trung. |
| How about starting with social media? | Đề xuất ý tưởng | Bắt đầu với mạng xã hội nhé? |
| Let's go for 20 per cent. | Đề xuất mục tiêu | Hướng đến 20 phần trăm nhé. |
| Why don't you ask Vanya? | Đề xuất cho người khác | Tại sao bạn không hỏi Vanya? |
| Shall we present it together? | Đề xuất cùng làm | Chúng ta cùng trình bày nhé? |
| Agreed. Good idea. | Đồng ý đề xuất | Đồng ý. Ý hay. |
| Good plan. | Đồng ý đề xuất | Kế hoạch hay. |

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase is used to make a suggestion to a group?
quizzes.hint_text: 'Let's...' is used to suggest something for a group (including the speaker).

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'How about starting with social media?' What is she doing?

options.A: Asking a question about the past.

quizzes.hint_text: 'How about...?' is a common way to make a suggestion.

quizzes.question_text: Which word shows that Noelia AGREES with Yuna's suggestion?
quizzes.hint_text: 'Sure' means yes, I agree.

quizzes.question_text: Complete: '___ we present it at the next team meeting together?'
quizzes.hint_text: 'Shall we...?' is used to suggest doing something together.

quizzes.question_text: 'Agreed. Good idea.' — What does this response mean?

options.B: I need more time to think.
options.C: I accept and support the suggestion.
options.D: I do not understand the suggestion.

quizzes.hint_text: 'Agreed' means you agree; 'Good idea' shows support.

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'Why don't you ask Vanya to help?' This is a suggestion for ___.
quizzes.hint_text: 'Why don't you...' is directed at the other person (Noelia).

quizzes.question_text: What is the DIFFERENCE between 'Let's go' and 'Why don't you go'?

options.B: 'Let's go' includes the speaker; 'Why don't you go' is for the other person only.
options.D: 'Why don't you go' is more polite.

quizzes.hint_text: 'Let's' = let us (includes everyone); 'Why don't you' = only for the listener.

quizzes.question_text: What does Noelia say to agree to letting Paul see the plan first?
quizzes.hint_text: Noelia accepts the suggestion with 'Sure, I'll do that.'

quizzes.question_text: How about ___ with the report? (Complete the suggestion correctly.)
quizzes.hint_text: 'How about' is followed by the -ing (gerund) form of the verb.

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'Good plan.' This is a response to ___.

options.A: Noelia's suggestion to present at the team meeting.
options.B: Yuna's own idea about social media.
options.D: Paul's request to review the plan.

quizzes.hint_text: Yuna says 'Good plan' after Noelia asks 'Shall we present it at the next team meeting together?'

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase is NOT used in the dialogue?

options.B: How about starting with social media?
options.C: I think we should try harder.
options.D: Shall we present it at the next team meeting?

quizzes.hint_text: 'I think we should...' does not appear in the dialogue.

quizzes.question_text: Noelia says 'We'll need some extra help to make it work.' What is she doing?

options.B: Explaining a requirement for the plan to succeed.
options.D: Suggesting they hire new staff immediately.

quizzes.hint_text: She is noting what is needed for the 20% goal to be achieved.

quizzes.question_text: Which set of phrases are ALL used to agree with a suggestion?

options.C: Why don't you / Let's / How about
options.D: Good plan / Why don't you / Let's

quizzes.hint_text: 'Sure', 'Agreed', and 'Good plan' are all used to accept a suggestion.

quizzes.question_text: What is the social media engagement target for next month?
quizzes.hint_text: Yuna suggests: 'let's go for 20 per cent for next month.'

quizzes.question_text: You want to suggest that your friend tries a new restaurant. Which is MOST natural?

options.A: You must go to the new restaurant.
options.B: How about trying the new restaurant?
options.C: I am suggesting the new restaurant.

quizzes.hint_text: 'How about + -ing' is the most natural and polite way to make this suggestion.

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'Why don't you ask Vanya to help you create a new social media plan?' What is Vanya's role here?

options.A: Vanya will present the plan at the meeting.
options.B: Vanya will help create the social media plan.
options.D: Vanya will manage the whole team.

quizzes.hint_text: Yuna suggests Vanya helps with creating the social media plan.

preparation_pages.title: Chuẩn bị (Preparation)
preparation_pages.body:
### Các cụm từ gặp gỡ người mới (Meeting New People Phrases)

Dùng những cụm từ này khi bạn gặp và giới thiệu bản thân với người mới:

- **Hi, I'm [name].** — Xin chào, tôi là [tên].
- **You must be [name].** — Chắc bạn là [tên]. (khi đã biết tên trước)
- **Nice to meet you.** — Rất vui được gặp bạn.
- **You too.** — Tôi cũng vậy. (Rất vui được gặp bạn cũng vậy.)
- **This is [name].** — Đây là [tên]. (giới thiệu người khác)
- **So how was your journey?** — Chuyến đi của bạn thế nào?
- **It's great to be here!** — Tuyệt khi được ở đây!
- **It's great to have you here.** — Tuyệt khi có bạn ở đây.
- **Welcome to the team!** — Chào mừng đến với nhóm!

> **Mẹo học:** 'Nice to meet you' và 'You too' là cặp câu thường đi với nhau khi hai người gặp lần đầu.

content.reading_passage:
## Đoạn hội thoại mẫu: Meeting New People

**Bối cảnh:** Vanya đến văn phòng mới. Emir được Paul nhờ đón Vanya. Sau đó Vanya gặp Yuna.

---

**Vanya:** Hi.

**Emir:** Hello.

**Vanya:** Are you Paul?

**Emir:** No, I'm not. But he asked me to meet you. I'm Emir. You must be Vanya.

**Vanya:** Yes, I am. Nice to meet you.

**Emir:** You too. Can I show you the office?

**Vanya:** Perfect. Thanks.

**Emir:** So how was your journey?

**Vanya:** It was fine and it's great to be here!

**Emir:** Well, it's great to have you here. Can I get you a coffee?

**Vanya:** Oh, yes, please.

*(Later...)*

**Emir:** Ah, Yuna, this is Vanya.

**Yuna:** Oh, hi, I'm Yuna. Nice to meet you. Welcome to the team!

---

### Các cụm từ nổi bật:

| Cụm từ | Cách dùng |
|--------|----------|
| Hi, I'm Yuna. | Giới thiệu bản thân |
| You must be Vanya. | Xác nhận tên người khác khi đã biết trước |
| Nice to meet you. | Nói khi gặp ai lần đầu |
| You too. | Phản hồi khi ai đó nói 'Nice to meet you' |
| This is Vanya. | Giới thiệu người thứ ba |
| How was your journey? | Hỏi thăm sau chuyến đi |
| It's great to be here! | Bày tỏ sự vui mừng khi đến nơi mới |
| Welcome to the team! | Chào đón thành viên mới |

quizzes.question_text: When you meet someone for the first time, you say:
quizzes.hint_text: 'Nice to meet you' is the standard phrase for first meetings.

quizzes.question_text: Emir says 'You must be Vanya.' He uses this phrase because:

options.B: Paul told him her name before she arrived.
options.D: He saw her name on a badge.

quizzes.hint_text: Emir says Paul 'asked me to meet you,' so Paul told him her name.

quizzes.question_text: What does 'You too.' mean after 'Nice to meet you'?

options.A: Me too — it is also nice to meet you.

quizzes.hint_text: 'You too' is a short way of saying 'Nice to meet you too.'

quizzes.question_text: Emir says 'Ah, Yuna, this is Vanya.' What is he doing?
quizzes.hint_text: 'This is [name]' is used to introduce one person to another.

quizzes.question_text: How does Vanya respond to 'So how was your journey?'

options.A: It's great to have you here.
options.B: It was fine and it's great to be here!

quizzes.hint_text: Vanya answers the question about her journey positively.

quizzes.question_text: 'Welcome to the team!' — Who says this?
quizzes.hint_text: Yuna says 'Nice to meet you. Welcome to the team!' when she meets Vanya.

quizzes.question_text: Vanya asks 'Are you Paul?' — Why does she ask this?

options.A: She wants to know if Paul works there.
options.B: She was expecting to meet Paul.
options.D: She needs to speak with Paul urgently.

quizzes.hint_text: Emir says 'Paul asked me to meet you,' suggesting Paul was supposed to be there.

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase welcomes someone to a new workplace?
quizzes.hint_text: 'Welcome to the team!' is specifically used to greet a new team member.

quizzes.question_text: After showing Vanya the office, Emir offers her a ___.
quizzes.hint_text: Emir asks 'Can I get you a coffee?'

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase is used to express happiness about arriving somewhere new?
quizzes.hint_text: 'It's great to be here!' expresses joy about being in a new place.

quizzes.question_text: Your new colleague arrives at the office for the first time. What is the BEST thing to say?

options.A: Do you know how to do this job?
options.B: Hi, I'm [your name]. Nice to meet you. Welcome to the team!

quizzes.hint_text: Introduce yourself, greet them, and welcome them to the team.

quizzes.question_text: Emir says 'It's great to have you here.' This phrase is said by ___.

options.B: the person who is already there welcoming the newcomer

quizzes.hint_text: Emir says this to Vanya — he is the host welcoming her.

quizzes.question_text: Which of these phrases is used to INTRODUCE a third person (someone else)?
quizzes.hint_text: 'This is [name]' introduces another person to someone.

quizzes.question_text:
Put the conversation in the correct order:
1. Nice to meet you.
2. Hi, I'm Ana.
3. You too.
4. You must be Yuna.
quizzes.hint_text: Introduce yourself → guess the other person's name → say nice to meet you → respond.

quizzes.question_text: What does Ana (the presenter) ask at the end of the lesson?

options.B: Do you like meeting new people?

quizzes.hint_text: The discussion question is 'Do you like meeting new people?'

quizzes.question_text: Which sentence is GRAMMATICALLY CORRECT for introducing yourself?
quizzes.hint_text: 'Hi, I'm [name]' is the natural and grammatically correct way to introduce yourself.

preparation_pages.title: Chuẩn bị (Preparation)
preparation_pages.body:
### Các cụm từ nói về người khác (Talking About Other People Phrases)

Dùng những cụm từ này khi bạn muốn nói về người khác:

- **Do you know [name]?** — Bạn có biết [tên] không?
- **Yes, I do.** — Có, tôi biết.
- **She's lovely, isn't she?** — Cô ấy dễ thương lắm, phải không?
- **I like her a lot.** — Tôi rất thích cô ấy.
- **She's very good at her job.** — Cô ấy rất giỏi công việc.
- **She's an excellent businessperson.** — Cô ấy là một người kinh doanh xuất sắc.
- **She always gets what she wants.** — Cô ấy luôn đạt được điều mình muốn.
- **Do you work with the digital team?** — Bạn có làm việc với nhóm kỹ thuật số không?

> **Mẹo học:** Khi mô tả người khác, hãy dùng tính từ tích cực như 'lovely', 'excellent', 'very good at' để nói lịch sự và chuyên nghiệp.

content.reading_passage:
## Đoạn hội thoại mẫu: Talking About Other People

**Bối cảnh:** Yuna hỏi thăm Noelia về dự án mới. Họ cùng nói về Anya Patel — người mà cả hai đều biết.

---

**Yuna:** How's your new project going?

**Noelia:** It's going really well.

**Yuna:** Great. Do you work with the digital team?

**Noelia:** Yes, I'm helping them with a new website.

**Yuna:** So, do you know Anya Patel?

**Noelia:** Yes, I do. I just emailed her.

**Yuna:** She's lovely, isn't she? I worked with her in my last job.

**Noelia:** Yeah, I like her a lot.

**Yuna:** She's very good at her job. She's an excellent businessperson.

**Noelia:** Really?

**Yuna:** Yeah, she always gets what she wants.

**Noelia:** Oh — I still need to discuss the contract with her.

**Yuna:** I see. OK —

**Noelia:** That's her!

**Yuna:** Good luck!

**Noelia:** Thanks.

**Noelia:** Hello, Anya. Noelia speaking!

---

### Cách mô tả người khác:

| Cụm từ | Nghĩa | Loại mô tả |
|--------|-------|------------|
| She's lovely. | Cô ấy dễ thương. | Tính cách chung |
| I like her a lot. | Tôi rất thích cô ấy. | Cảm xúc cá nhân |
| She's very good at her job. | Cô ấy rất giỏi việc. | Năng lực nghề nghiệp |
| She's an excellent businessperson. | Cô ấy là doanh nhân xuất sắc. | Đánh giá cao |
| She always gets what she wants. | Cô ấy luôn đạt được điều muốn. | Đặc điểm cá nhân |

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'She's lovely, isn't she?' What is Yuna doing?

options.B: Saying something nice about Anya and asking for agreement.
options.C: Telling Noelia to work with Anya.

quizzes.hint_text: The tag question 'isn't she?' asks Noelia to agree with the description.

quizzes.question_text: How does Noelia respond to 'Do you know Anya Patel?'
quizzes.hint_text: Noelia confirms she knows Anya by saying 'Yes, I do.'

quizzes.question_text: 'She's very good at her job.' This phrase describes Anya's ___.
quizzes.hint_text: 'Good at her job' refers to her skills and professional competence.

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'She's an excellent businessperson.' Which word means very, very good?
quizzes.hint_text: 'Excellent' is a strong adjective meaning extremely good.

quizzes.question_text: Why does Noelia say 'Oh — I still need to discuss the contract with her'?

options.A: She is worried because Anya is difficult to work with.
options.B: She remembers she has unfinished business with Anya.
options.C: She wants Yuna to introduce her to Anya.
options.D: She has already finished the contract.

quizzes.hint_text: After hearing 'she always gets what she wants,' Noelia remembers the contract discussion.

quizzes.question_text: What does 'I like her a lot' mean?

options.B: I have a strong positive feeling toward her.

quizzes.hint_text: 'I like her a lot' expresses a strong positive personal feeling.

quizzes.question_text: What is Noelia doing for the digital team?

options.B: Helping them with a new website.

quizzes.hint_text: Noelia says 'Yes, I'm helping them with a new website.'

quizzes.question_text: Where did Yuna work with Anya before?
quizzes.hint_text: Yuna says 'I worked with her in my last job.'

quizzes.question_text: Yuna says 'She always gets what she wants.' This suggests Anya is ___.
quizzes.hint_text: In context, this means Anya is very effective at achieving her goals — a compliment.

quizzes.question_text: 'She's lovely, isn't she?' — The tag question 'isn't she?' is added to ___.

options.A: ask if Noelia has met Anya.
options.B: invite Noelia to agree with the statement.
options.D: ask for more information about Anya.

quizzes.hint_text: Tag questions like 'isn't she?' or 'don't you?' invite the listener to agree.

quizzes.question_text: Which phrase is NOT used to describe Anya in the dialogue?

options.B: She's very good at her job.

quizzes.hint_text: 'She's a very friendly person' does not appear in the dialogue.

quizzes.question_text: At the end, Noelia says 'Hello, Anya. Noelia speaking!' What is she doing?

options.C: Introducing herself to Anya in person.

quizzes.hint_text: '[Name] speaking' is a standard phrase used when answering or making a phone call.

quizzes.question_text: You want to ask a colleague if they know someone at your company. Which is MOST natural?

options.A: Are you knowing Tom from accounts?
options.B: Do you know Tom from accounts?

quizzes.hint_text: 'Do you know...?' is the correct form. 'Know' is not used in the continuous form.

quizzes.question_text: Yuna asks 'How's your new project going?' This means she wants to know ___.

options.B: the current progress or status of the project.
options.C: who is working on the project.

quizzes.hint_text: 'How's it going?' is a question about progress or current status.

quizzes.question_text: Which set of phrases are all used to describe someone positively?

options.A: She's lovely / She's very good at her job / She's an excellent businessperson
options.B: Do you know her? / Yes, I do / I just emailed her
options.C: She always gets what she wants / I still need to discuss the contract / Good luck!
options.D: How's your project? / It's going well / Do you work with the digital team?

quizzes.hint_text: All three phrases in option A are positive descriptions of a person.

quizzes.question_text: How does Noelia know Anya Patel?

options.B: Yuna introduced them at a meeting.
options.C: Noelia is helping the digital team and has been in contact with Anya.
options.D: They worked together in a previous job.

quizzes.hint_text: Noelia says 'Yes, I'm helping them with a new website' and 'I just emailed her.'
